name: Camino Primitivo Trail: Bodenaya to Salas
name_original: Bodenaya a Salas por el Camino Primitivo
description: Hike the moderate 9.6-mile Camino Primitivo Trail from Bodenaya to Salas, gaining 1548 feet through scenic Asturian countryside.
region_intro: Hiking around Salas, Asturias, offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and dense woodlands. The region is traversed by historical routes, including sections of the Camino Primitivo, providing a mix of cultural and natural exploration. Elevations are generally moderate, with paths winding through rural villages and past natural features like waterfalls. Best hiking trails around Salas The most popular hiking route is Camino Primitivo Trail: Bodenaya to Salas, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate path follows a historic pilgrimage route through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Salas Historic Town Center – Nonaya Waterfall loop from Salas, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route combines exploration of the historic town center with natural scenery leading to a waterfall. Local hikers also love the Stage 2: Grau/Grado - Salas, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and villages, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Hiking around Salas is defined by river valleys, historic Camino paths, and forested hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy day hikes to more challenging routes. The routes in Salas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 90 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Salas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Salas?
A: Yes, Salas offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048347834/stage-2-grau-grado-salas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stage 2: Grau/Grado - Salas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.5-mile (7.3 km) trail that takes about 2 hours to complete, leading through rural landscapes and villages.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes around Salas?
A: Hikes around Salas vary in duration. Many popular routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1048346815/camino-primitivo-trail-bodenaya-to-salas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Primitivo Trail: Bodenaya to Salas&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 4.5 hours. Shorter options, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5341137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas Historic Town Center – Nonaya Waterfall loop from Salas&lt;/a&gt;, are typically completed in about 2.5 hours. There are also longer, more challenging routes that can take over 6 hours.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Salas?
A: Salas has trails suitable for families, especially those looking for moderate distances and varied scenery. Routes that combine town exploration with natural features, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5341137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas Historic Town Center – Nonaya Waterfall loop from Salas&lt;/a&gt;, are often good choices for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Salas?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Salas region, especially on rural paths and natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected areas, and to check specific route regulations if available.

Q: Are there more challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Salas?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Salas offers more demanding routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38188866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casazorrina Bridge – Water Fountain loop from Villazón&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 14.7-mile (23.7 km) trail with significant elevation gain, typically taking over 6.5 hours to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Salas?
A: The best time for hiking in Salas is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, though some days might be warmer. Winter offers quieter trails, but conditions can be wetter and colder.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Salas?
A: Yes, Salas features several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5341137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salas Historic Town Center – Nonaya Waterfall loop from Salas&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to start and end in the same location while exploring both the town and natural scenery.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Salas?
A: While hiking around Salas, you can encounter various natural and historical attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/865581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nonaya Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural feature. You might also come across historical religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5125287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of St. Mary Major in Salas&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Regalina Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Salas?
A: The trails in Salas are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, which includes rolling hills, river valleys, and dense woodlands, as well as the historical routes like sections of the Camino Primitivo.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Salas?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, most popular hiking routes in and around Salas, especially those starting from villages or town centers, generally have accessible parking options nearby. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there hiking opportunities in Salas during winter?
A: Hiking in Salas is possible during winter, though conditions can be cooler and wetter. The lower elevation trails and those through villages are generally accessible. Always check weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out, and dress in appropriate layers for warmth and rain protection.

Q: Can I find scenic viewpoints on the trails around Salas?
A: Yes, the varied terrain of Salas, characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, offers numerous opportunities for scenic viewpoints. Many trails provide panoramic vistas of the Asturian countryside, especially from higher elevations or along ridge lines.
tips: Small waterfall, which is only 250 meters away from the Way of St. James. Nice place for a little break.
Very simple fountain, built in stone, with a stone plate in which the spout is located, the water falling into a stone basin.

A pretty 17th century church. Especially notable is the altarpiece of the main altar and the mausoleum of the inquisitor Valdés Salas. If you're lucky, the local tour guide can give you an explanation of the temple.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Camino Primitivo Trail: Bodenaya to Salas?
faqs.answer: The estimated hiking time for this 15.4 km section is around 4 hours and 30 minutes. However, this can vary based on your pace, stops for rest, and time spent exploring points of interest like the Nonaya Waterfall or the town of Salas.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Bodenaya to Salas section of the Camino Primitivo?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally moderate, featuring a mix of paved and unpaved sections, including forest paths and stretches alongside roads. You'll encounter considerable uphill walking, particularly if hiking from Salas towards Bodenaya, but climbs are usually gradual. Be prepared for potentially muddy areas, especially after rain.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route from Bodenaya to Salas?
faqs.answer: This section offers beautiful Asturian countryside views, with mountains, forests, and small villages. Key highlights include the Nonaya Waterfall (a short detour), the simple Fuente de Pain, and the charming medieval town of Salas, home to the Collegiate Church of St. Mary Major and the Palacio de Valdés Salas.

faqs.question: Is this section of the Camino Primitivo suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, while it involves a moderate amount of uphill walking and requires good fitness, the paths are generally accessible and not overly technical. It's suitable for various skill levels, including beginners who are prepared for a steady hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this section of the Camino Primitivo?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is typically during the spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) when the weather is milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be pleasant but may be warmer, while winter can bring colder temperatures and more challenging conditions, including mud.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay overnight along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bodenaya is famous for its 'donativo' albergue, known for its warm welcome and meals. Salas, at the end of this section, is a larger town with various accommodation options, cafes, and restaurants where you can resupply and rest.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this section is part of the larger Camino Primitivo route. It also overlaps with sections of 'Etapa 3: Salas - Tinéu' and briefly with 'Etapa 2: Grau/Grado - Salas'.

faqs.question: Is the Camino Primitivo Trail: Bodenaya to Salas dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Camino Primitivo generally welcomes dogs, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through villages, farmlands with livestock, or near roads. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them. Check specific albergues or accommodations in Salas for their pet policies.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to hike this section of the Camino Primitivo. The trail is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail, and is public transport available?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Salas, which is a larger town at one end of this section. For Bodenaya, parking might be more limited, often near the albergue or village center. Public transport options to and from Salas are available, connecting it to larger towns in Asturias, which can be useful for starting or ending your hike.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for hiking this section?
faqs.answer: Many pilgrims intentionally plan their stages to include a stay at the Albergue de Bodenaya. It's a highly recommended 'donativo' albergue known for its exceptional hospitality and community atmosphere, making it a memorable part of the Camino experience.

waypoints.name: Collegiate Church of St. Mary Major in Salas
